message:
chore(beads,harness): update issue tracker state and expand E2E harness

- Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue status transitions
- Update ffs-harness E2E test and main entrypoint with in-progress changes
  from concurrent agent sessions

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-32yn.3","title":"Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)","description":"# Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when critical metadata structures are corrupted. Metadata corruption is catastrophic — it can make the entire filesystem unreadable.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Superblock corruption
- Corrupt primary superblock fields (magic number, block count, inode count)
- Expected: mount fails with clear error, backup superblock used if available

### Scenario 2: Group Descriptor Table corruption
- Corrupt GDT entries (block bitmap location, inode bitmap, inode table)
- Expected: affected block groups inaccessible, other groups still readable

### Scenario 3: Inode corruption
- Corrupt individual inode fields (size, block count, extent tree root)
- Expected: affected file inaccessible, other files intact

### Scenario 4: Inode bitmap corruption
- Corrupt inode allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, defensive read behavior

### Scenario 5: Block bitmap corruption
- Corrupt block allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, read-only degradation

### Scenario 6: Extent tree internal node corruption
- Corrupt B-tree internal node in extent tree
- Expected: file partially readable (extents before corruption accessible)

### Recovery Testing
- For each scenario: verify RaptorQ repair (if metadata has 2x overhead) can recover
- If repair impossible: verify graceful degradation and clear error reporting

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Each metadata corruption type produces appropriate error response
- [ ] No panics from corrupted metadata
- [ ] Checksums detect corruption before use (no silent data misinterpretation)
- [ ] RaptorQ metadata repair works for recoverable cases
- [ ] Evidence ledger records all detected corruption
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T04:10:22.007028484Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fault","metadata"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:24.252718351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32yn.4","title":"Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)","description":"# Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior under degraded I/O performance — simulating slow disks, network storage latency, or I/O congestion.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Uniform slow I/O
- Add 50ms latency to every read/write operation
- Expected: filesystem remains functional, FUSE operations complete (slowly)
- Measure: FUSE operation timeout handling, queue depths

### Scenario 2: Intermittent I/O stalls
- Random 500ms-2s stalls on 5% of I/O operations
- Expected: no deadlocks, backpressure activates, operations eventually complete

### Scenario 3: I/O bandwidth limit
- Limit to 10MB/s read, 5MB/s write (simulating NAS or USB)
- Expected: FlushDaemon adapts, no OOM from dirty block accumulation

### Scenario 4: I/O timeout
- I/O operations that take > 30s return ETIMEDOUT
- Expected: operation fails gracefully, no resource leak

### Scenario 5: Progressive degradation
- Start with normal I/O, gradually increase latency over 60s
- Expected: backpressure system activates, evidence ledger records degradation

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Pluggable I/O layer with configurable delay, bandwidth limit, timeout
- Delay injection: uniform, gaussian, or bimodal distribution
- Integration: inject at ffs-block I/O boundary

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] No deadlocks under any I/O degradation scenario
- [ ] Backpressure correctly activates under I/O stalls
- [ ] No OOM from unbounded dirty block accumulation during slow I/O
- [ ] Operations fail gracefully on timeout (no resource leak)
- [ ] All scenarios CI-executable with deterministic delays","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26.362143810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault","io","throttle"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.

- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32yn.5","title":"Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes","description":"# Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes

## GOAL
Run 500 deterministic crash-replay scenarios: each generates a random sequence of filesystem operations, injects a crash at a random point, recovers, and verifies consistency.

## DESIGN

### Operation Generator
- Random sequence of: create, write, read, rename, unlink, mkdir, rmdir, fsync
- Operations parameterized: file size, directory depth, name length
- Deterministic: seeded PRNG produces reproducible sequences
- Length: 100-1000 operations per schedule

### Crash Point Selection
- For each schedule, select 1-3 random crash points
- Crash point = operation index + I/O sub-operation (e.g., crash during 3rd block write of operation #47)
- Ensures coverage of: mid-write, mid-fsync, mid-rename, mid-unlink crashes

### Recovery and Verification
After each crash:
1. Mount filesystem (triggers crash recovery)
2. Full filesystem walk: verify all accessible files are readable
3. Consistency check: no dangling inodes, no double-allocated blocks
4. Committed data check: all fsync'd data intact
5. Evidence ledger check: recovery actions logged

### Reporting
- Per-schedule: pass/fail, crash point, recovery time, evidence summary
- Aggregate: pass rate, mean recovery time, most common failure mode
- Any failure: save full reproducer (seed, schedule, crash point)

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Test harness: Rust test binary with configurable seed range
- Parallelizable: each schedule is independent (run on multiple cores)
- CI: run all 500 schedules on every PR (target: < 10 minutes total)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] 500 schedules generated and executed
- [ ] 100% pass rate (zero consistency violations after recovery)
- [ ] All fsync'd data preserved after crash
- [ ] Failure reproducers saved for any failing schedule
- [ ] Total runtime < 10 minutes on CI hardware
- [ ] Every schedule deterministic (same seed = same result)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:22:24.890498854Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T18:22:24.890472104Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","deterministic","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:22.037666789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":126,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B

- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-32yn.6","title":"Conformance: fsx-style stress test with corruption injection","description":"# Conformance: fsx-style stress test with corruption injection

## GOAL
Implement an fsx-style (File System eXerciser) stress test that performs random file operations while continuously verifying data integrity. Extended with FrankenFS-specific corruption injection to test self-healing under stress.

## BACKGROUND
fsx (from LTP/xfstests) is the gold standard for filesystem stress testing. It performs random reads, writes, truncates, and mmaps on a file, maintaining an in-memory reference copy. After each operation, it verifies the file matches the reference. Any mismatch is a bug.

## DESIGN

### Operations (weighted random selection)
- write(offset, length, data): 40%
- read(offset, length) + verify: 25%
- truncate(length): 10%
- fsync(): 10%
- fallocate(offset, length): 5%
- punch_hole(offset, length): 5%
- close + reopen: 5%

### Verification
- In-memory reference buffer tracks expected file content
- After each read: compare against reference
- Periodically: close file, reopen, read entire file, compare against reference
- After unmount + remount: verify entire file against reference

### Corruption Injection Extension
- Every N operations (configurable, default 1000): inject random block corruption
- Wait for scrub daemon to detect and repair
- Verify file still matches reference after repair
- Evidence ledger check: repair logged correctly

### Parameters
- File size: configurable (default 64MB)
- Operation count: configurable (default 100000)
- Seed: deterministic
- Duration mode: run for N seconds instead of N operations

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Zero data mismatches across 100K operations
- [ ] Survives corruption injection + repair cycles
- [ ] Deterministic with seed (reproducible failures)
- [ ] Runs in < 5 minutes for default parameters
- [ ] Clear failure report with exact operation that triggered mismatch","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:07.106145900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:23:13.326148462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fsx","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.5","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:23.139598637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:07.106145900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":125,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

-{"id":"bd-32yn.7","title":"E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets","description":"# E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets

## GOAL
Run the standard Linux filesystem test suite (xfstests/fstests) against FrankenFS FUSE mount to verify compatibility with expected filesystem semantics.

## BACKGROUND
xfstests (github.com/kdave/xfstests) is the authoritative filesystem test suite used by all major Linux filesystems. It contains:
- generic/: tests applicable to all POSIX filesystems (~600 tests)
- ext4/: tests specific to ext4 semantics (~100 tests)

## APPROACH

### Phase 1: Generic Subset
Run xfstests generic/ tests that apply to FUSE filesystems. Exclude tests requiring:
- Direct I/O (O_DIRECT) — FUSE may not support
- Filesystem-specific ioctls
- Quota management
- ACLs (unless we implement them)
- Kernel-specific features (fiemap, etc.)

Expected pass rate: start with identifying which tests pass, create tracking for regressions.

### Phase 2: ext4 Subset
Run xfstests ext4/ tests. Many require mkfs.ext4 features we may not support yet.
Subset: tests covering basic file operations, directory operations, extent handling.

### Infrastructure
- xfstests requires: TEST_DEV (FrankenFS FUSE mount), SCRATCH_DEV (second mount for temp data)
- Wrapper script: configure xfstests for FrankenFS, run selected tests, capture results
- CI integration: run on every PR, track pass/fail count over time

### Known Exclusions
- Tests requiring root (CAP_SYS_ADMIN)
- Tests requiring kernel features not available in FUSE
- Tests for features we explicitly don't implement (quotas, ACLs, etc.)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] xfstests infrastructure configured for FrankenFS
- [ ] Generic test subset identified and documented
- [ ] ext4 test subset identified and documented
- [ ] CI integration: test results tracked per commit
- [ ] Pass rate monotonically increases over time
- [ ] Regression: any previously passing test that fails blocks merge","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.178500479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2e","xfstests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":124,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths

 {"id":"bd-32yn.8","title":"Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness","description":"# Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Testing and Fault Injection epic (bd-32yn). The epic has many E2E/integration test beads (bd-32yn.1 through bd-32yn.7) but NO unit tests for the fault injection framework itself -- the pluggable I/O layer, failure point registration, deterministic replay, and crash-point scheduling.

## TEST PLAN

### Pluggable I/O Layer Tests
1. FaultInjector wraps BlockDevice transparently (no faults = passthrough)
2. fail_at_byte(offset) triggers fault at exact byte
3. fail_on_read(block) returns injected error
4. fail_on_write(block) returns injected error
5. Fault cleared after trigger (one-shot mode)
6. Persistent fault mode (fault on every access)

### Deterministic Replay Tests
7. Same seed produces same fault sequence
8. Fault sequence is reproducible across runs
9. Fault log captures all injected faults with timestamps

### Crash Point Tests
10. Crash point registered at specific operation
11. Crash point fires at correct moment (before/after fsync)
12. Recovery after crash point: filesystem mounts cleanly

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 12 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Fault injected (debug): fault_type, target_block, operation
- Crash point fired (info): crash_point_id, operation_in_flight

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 12 tests pass
2. Framework is reusable across all fault injection beads
3. Deterministic replay verified","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:24.326422449Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["faultinjection","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-375","title":"EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount

## PURPOSE
Enable read-only btrfs FUSE mounting, achieving parity with ext4 read-only support. Currently btrfs metadata parsing is at 35% (7/20).

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md, btrfs gaps:
- btrfs transaction parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs delayed refs parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs scrub parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- FUSE mount for btrfs: NOT WORKING

However, significant foundation exists:
- Superblock decode: ✅
- B-tree header decode: ✅
- Leaf item metadata decode: ✅
- Internal node parsing: ✅
- sys_chunk mapping: ✅
- Read-only tree walk: ✅
- BtrfsContext in ffs-core: ✅

The remaining gap is wiring tree walking and extent resolution into `FsOps` and then into the FUSE surface.

## CURRENT STATE
- `ffs-ondisk` has comprehensive btrfs parsing
- `ffs-btrfs` provides `walk_tree`
- `ffs-core` has a `BtrfsContext` stub
- `ffs-fuse` operations currently route to ext4-only `FsOps`

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement `BtrfsFsOps` (like `Ext4FsOps` but for btrfs)
2. Wire `ffs-cli mount` path to btrfs ops
3. Implement btrfs inode -> extent resolution sufficient for read-only file reads
4. Add E2E coverage for btrfs mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount btrfs.img /mnt` works read-only
2. Basic file operations work: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`
3. Subvolume visibility (at least default subvol)
4. Performance baseline established (hyperfine) for inspect + mount smoke
5. btrfs metadata parsing reaches 60%+ parity

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk) - need fixtures/goldens
- btrfs golden fixtures (bd-2jk.2)

## EXPLICIT V1 EXCLUSIONS (per PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md)
- Multi-device / RAID: OUT OF SCOPE
- Compression: OUT OF SCOPE
- Send/receive: OUT OF SCOPE
- Write support: OUT OF SCOPE

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §4-6 (btrfs behavior)
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §2 (btrfs format)

## Success Criteria
1. `ffs inspect <btrfs.img>` and `ffs mount <btrfs.img>` work on the golden fixtures without panics.
2. `scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_ro_smoke.sh` passes (or skips cleanly when FUSE unavailable) and produces actionable logs.
3. Unit tests cover:
   - inode lookup by key
   - directory enumeration ordering/offsets
   - extent resolution for file reads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:56:58.365480057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22.338387217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","fuse"],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-375","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-375.1","title":"Implement BtrfsFsOps for FUSE operations","description":"# Implement BtrfsFsOps for FUSE operations

## GOAL
Add **read-only** btrfs support behind the existing `ffs-core::FsOps` trait so FUSE can mount btrfs images (bd-375.2).

## CURRENT REALITY (CODE)
- `ffs-core` already parses btrfs superblock + sys_chunk mapping at open time (`BtrfsContext`).
- `ffs-core::OpenFs` already has tree-walk helpers (`walk_btrfs_tree`, `walk_btrfs_root_tree`).
- `ffs-btrfs` can walk trees and yields raw leaf entries (`BtrfsLeafEntry { key, data }`).
- What’s missing is decoding leaf payloads into the minimum set of item types needed for VFS operations.

## SCOPE
Implement the minimal btrfs read-only VFS surface:
- `getattr`: inode -> `InodeAttr`
- `lookup`: (parent ino, name) -> child inode
- `readdir`: enumerate dir entries with stable offsets
- `read`: read file bytes via extent resolution

## ARCHITECTURE CHOICE
Prefer **dispatch inside `impl FsOps for OpenFs`** based on `self.flavor`:
- ext4 path: keep existing behavior
- btrfs path: add new btrfs implementations

Alternative (acceptable): introduce a separate `BtrfsFsOps` struct and use it from CLI mount.

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN
1. Define/implement decoding helpers for the needed btrfs item payloads.
   - Where: `crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs` (higher-level) and/or `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` (pure parsing helpers)
   - Must cover at least:
     - ROOT_ITEM (to find the FS tree root)
     - INODE_ITEM
     - DIR_ITEM / DIR_INDEX
     - EXTENT_DATA (enough to support reading regular files)
2. Add search helpers that operate over a leaf-entry stream:
   - find first matching key
   - iterate a key prefix/range (for directory enumeration)
3. Implement btrfs `FsOps` methods:
   - `getattr`: lookup inode item and map to `InodeAttr`
   - `lookup`: find dir item for `name`, return child ino attr
   - `readdir`: enumerate dir items; offsets must be consistent across calls
   - `read`: resolve file offset to physical via extent mapping + device reads

## FILES TO MODIFY
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` (btrfs path inside `impl FsOps for OpenFs`, plus helper methods)
- `crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs` and/or `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` (item decoding)
- `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs` and new targeted harness tests for item decoding using fixtures from bd-2jk.2

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit tests for each decoded item type (parse known bytes -> expected fields).
2. Harness tests that use committed sparse fixtures to validate:
   - inode lookup
   - directory enumeration ordering
   - extent resolution invariants (at least one file read path)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. btrfs `FsOps` paths compile and are reachable (no stub/bail).
2. `getattr/lookup/readdir/read` work for the btrfs reference image used by bd-375.3.
3. Errors for unsupported btrfs features are explicit and actionable.
4. All new behavior is covered by tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:30.943924807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:54:02.245759056Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:54:02.245692622Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","harness","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-375.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2jk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:48.566411670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-375.1","depends_on_id":"bd-375","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:30.943924807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
